mission.title: Install and Configure KubeRay
mission.description: KubeRay is an open-source Kubernetes operator that simplifies the deployment and management of Ray applications on Kubernetes. It is ideal for users looking to run distributed machine learning workloads efficiently. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes and potential complexity in managing Ray clusters.

steps.title: Add the KubeRay Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeRay Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeRay easily.

```bash

helm repo add kuberay https://ray-project.github.io/kuberay

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the KubeRay Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeRay CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for KubeRay to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/raycluster.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayjob.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/rayservice.yaml

```

> Installs the RayCluster CRD.
> Installs the RayJob CRD.
> Installs the RayService CRD.

steps.title: Install KubeRay using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs KubeRay on your Kubernetes cluster using the Helm chart.

```bash

helm install kuberay kuberay/kuberay --version 1.5.1 --namespace kuberay --create-namespace

```

> Installs KubeRay in the 'kuberay' namespace.

steps.title: Verify KubeRay installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the KubeRay components are running correctly in your cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

kubectl get crd

```

> Lists all pods in the 'kuberay' namespace to verify installation.
> Verifies that the CRDs are installed.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeRay
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeRay and its associated resources from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all Ray clusters and jobs managed by KubeRay, leading to potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kuberay --namespace kuberay

kubectl delete namespace kuberay

```

> Uninstalls KubeRay from the specified namespace.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeRay
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step will upgrade KubeRay to the latest version and verify the installation afterward.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kuberay -o json > kuberay-backup.json

helm upgrade kuberay kuberay/kuberay --version 1.5.1 --namespace kuberay

kubectl get pods -n kuberay

```

> Backup current KubeRay pod configurations.
> Upgrades KubeRay to the specified version.
> Verifies that all KubeRay pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kuberay -o json > kuberay-backup.json

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KubeRay operator not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KubeRay operator may not be installed or running.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl get pods -n kuberay' to check the status of the KubeRay operator pod.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'RayCluster not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The RayCluster CRD may not be installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRD using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ray-project/kuberay/v1.5.1/deploy/crds/raycluster.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'Pending' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources in the cluster to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Check resource availability and consider scaling your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Job failed with exit code 1'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The RayJob may have encountered an error during execution.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the RayJob pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kuberay'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Ray features and improved stability of the operator.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KubeRay pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n kuberay'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
